title: Customs and Excise Officer
description: Customs and Excise Officers are responsible for enforcing customs and excise laws, preventing smuggling, assessing and collecting duties and taxes, and facilitating legitimate trade. They play a crucial role in protecting the economic interests and security of India.
content: Customs and Excise Officers work for the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC), a department under the Ministry of Finance. They are involved in various activities such as inspecting goods, conducting audits, investigating offenses, and managing customs stations and ports. This career requires strong analytical skills, integrity, and the ability to work under pressure.

financialFeasibility.note: The UPSC Civil Services Examination is accessible to candidates from all economic backgrounds. Coaching and study materials can be expensive, but affordable options are available.

sectorScore.remarks: This is primarily a government sector job. Opportunities in the private sector are limited to consultancy roles related to customs and excise regulations.

relatedCareers.reason: Similar skills in policy implementation and administration are required.
relatedCareers.title: Indian Administrative Service Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Enforcement of laws and maintaining order are common aspects.
relatedCareers.title: Police Officer

relatedCareers.reason: Knowledge of finance and taxation is beneficial.
relatedCareers.title: Chartered Accountant

genderDistribution.note: While historically male-dominated, there is increasing female representation in the Indian Revenue Service.
